readerView.agentInstruction: Do not read, parse, or update this HTML file during normal task work. Agents coordinate by updating board.json only.

transitionApi.apiLog: task-board/task-board-api.log records every GET and POST API request as JSON Lines with timestamp, method, endpoint, task ID, agentName, status, and error details.
transitionApi.agentInstruction: When the local task-board backend is running, prefer these endpoints for task creation, edits, status changes, and cleanup so claims and moves are atomic. If the backend is not running, update board.json directly using the same workflow rules.
transitionApi.agentName: Every agent must register at chat start with POST /api/register-agent (personalName, model, role) to receive an agentId, then include that agentId in every later API payload.
transitionApi.titleValidation: Every task must have a real, nonblank title. Untitled, Untitled task, and blank titles are invalid; the API rejects them and the viewer hides malformed records.

roleSeparation.planner: Planner is planning-only. It records requirements in board.json, but never switches into Worker or Reviewer behavior in the same chat.
roleSeparation.worker: Worker execution starts only in a separate Worker chat or when the owner explicitly starts an agent with the Worker role.
roleSeparation.reviewer: Reviewer review starts only in a separate Reviewer chat or when the owner explicitly starts an agent with the Reviewer role.
roleSeparation.hardRule: Planner must not review completed work, claim tasks, edit product code, run implementation, or make code changes. Reviewer may update only board.json (plus reference images when recorded on a follow-up task). Reviewer must not plan general work, claim todo tasks, edit code, edit docs, edit the HTML viewer, run implementation, or make code changes.

requirements: Exercise reload JSON, auto refresh, file load, lane collapse, task collapse, logs tray, workflow panel, settings panel, planner chat, dispatch controls, pause controls, and archive tray controls where safe.
Verify hover and focus behavior for active/stale agent chips and log preview popovers.
Check keyboard behavior including Tab navigation, visible focus, Enter/Space activation where applicable, and Escape/close behavior for trays and chat.
Avoid unsafe mutations unless using a fixture or explicitly disposable temporary tasks. Record any skipped destructive actions and why.
acceptanceCriteria: Browser QA covers the listed controls with screenshots, notes, or a concise evidence log.
No unsafe mutation is made to live coordination tasks during QA, or every temporary task is cleaned up afterward with evidence.
Any broken control, keyboard trap, missing focus state, clipped popover, or confusing state transition is documented with reproduction steps.
The QA result explicitly states whether BrowserOS/in-app browser access was available and what coverage remains if not.

todo.title: Verify responsive layout of viewer trays and board lanes
todo.summary: Perform desktop and mobile visual QA of the task-board viewer trays, board lanes, controls, and agent popovers.
requirements: Inspect the viewer in representative desktop and mobile viewport sizes.
Check the topbar, board lanes, archive tray, workflow tray, settings tray, agent logs tray, planner chat, and agent popovers.
Verify text fit, side-tab usability, tray positioning, mobile overflow, visual hierarchy, and tap target sizes.
Record screenshots or clear evidence notes for each inspected viewport and surface.
acceptanceCriteria: Desktop and mobile evidence shows no overlapping text, clipped controls, inaccessible side tabs, unreadable popovers, or broken tap targets.
Side trays and planner chat remain usable without blocking core board interaction unexpectedly.
Any responsive defects found are documented with viewport, reproduction steps, and suggested follow-up scope.
Keyboard-visible focus and Escape/close behavior are checked for responsive tray states where applicable.

done.title: Verify stale-agent cleanup and messaging
done.summary: Improve or verify how the viewer and backend present stale agent records so owners can distinguish active work from stale historical state.
requirements: Inspect how stale active-agent records are detected, reported, and displayed in system status and the viewer.
Clarify owner-facing messaging so stale records do not look like active work or unresolved board locks.
Determine whether stale records should be manually clearable, automatically aged out, or retained only as audit history, and implement the chosen behavior if code changes are needed.
Preserve useful audit/history information while avoiding misleading active-agent counts.
acceptanceCriteria: The owner can identify stale agents and understand whether action is required from the viewer or system-status output.
Stale records do not create ambiguity about current board health, active work, or active locks.
If a clear/ignore/age-out action exists, it is safe, discoverable, and does not remove live active agents.
The behavior is verified against a stale-agent scenario like the auditor evidence: system status watch due to stale worker ada / agt_89de2a01.

inspectionTargets.label: System status API stale-history scenario
inspectionTargets.state: Request while stale ada / agt_89de2a01 and stale planner records exist, with no stale active task lock.
inspectionTargets.notes: Confirm status is ok, warnings is empty, notices explains stale records are history, agents.staleWithLocks is 0, and agents.staleHistory is 2.

inspectionTargets.label: Viewer system overview stale messaging
inspectionTargets.state: Open the viewer with stale history records present and the updated backend running on port 4177.
inspectionTargets.notes: Confirm the Agents card separates Stale locks from Stale history, the notice is informational, and history-only stale records do not make active-agent counts look live. Browser surface was unavailable in this worker session, so use this as the visual review target.

done.notes:
Source: workflow/handoffs/site-auditor-to-planner.md. Auditor evidence: /api/system-status returned status watch with stale agent warnings, and /api/agents showed stale worker ada / agt_89de2a01 tied to TASK-20260630-001.

Implemented stale-agent status classification. /api/system-status now distinguishes stale history from stale records that still own live claimed/reviewing locks, returns staleRecords/stalePolicy metadata, and only warns when a stale lock needs action. The viewer Agents card now shows Stale locks and Stale history separately and renders history-only stale records as a neutral notice instead of a health warning. Verified python3 -m py_compile task-board/server.py, inline viewer script parse, served viewer contains the new UI strings, and live /api/system-status reports ok with staleWithLocks 0 and staleHistory 2 for the ada scenario. In-app browser was unavailable, so rendered click-through remains the reviewer inspection target.

Review approved (2026-07-01T19:48:12-07:00): Approved. Verified /api/system-status reports ok with warnings empty, notices explaining stale history, agents.staleWithLocks 0, and agents.staleHistory 2 for the ada/planner stale-history scenario. Inspected server stale-agent classification and confirmed an in-memory stale-lock spot check marks active claimed/reviewing locks as actionable while history-only records remain non-actionable. Verified task-board/server.py compiles, task-board/viewer.html inline script parses, served viewer contains the Stale locks/Stale history labels, and CSS renders notices separately from warnings. Rendered browser click-through was unavailable because the in-app browser backend list was empty.
